extra.reasoning: The output acuity is computed from five rules, none of which reference back_pain as a condition: r1 (dizziness->urgent, pri2), r2 (slurred_speech->urgent, pri2), r3 (leg_swelling->emergent, pri3), r4 (sweating->emergent, pri3), r5 (vision_loss->urgent, pri2), with default routine. Since back_pain appears in no rule's condition, the set of matching rules — and thus the resolved acuity — depends only on {dizziness, slurred_speech, leg_swelling, sweating, vision_loss}. Toggling back_pain from false to true while holding all other inputs fixed leaves the matching-rule set identical, so acuity_after equals acuity_before for every input. The monotonicity property requires acuity_after >= acuity_before; equality satisfies this (equal is not lower). No input can make setting back_pain true lower the acuity because back_pain has zero effect on the output. The property holds over the entire input space.
